TextENGNew: ``Come on, pull yourself together.@k@vS16/45/DS43070279. Good grief...``
After pulling the young man from the waterfall basin and laying him on the rocky bank of the stream, I slapped his cheeks lightly.
changeReason: New translation specifies 'stream' (沢) instead of 'marsh' and adds 'lightly' for the slapping, more accurately reflecting the Japanese onomatopoeia ぺしぺし

TextENGNew: ...But whether he was unconscious or simply dazed, his eyes refused to open, and the only response I received was a feeble groan.
changeReason: New translation adds 'whether he was unconscious or simply dazed' (気を失っているのか), a conditional clause present in the Japanese but absent from the original

TextENGNew: ``What a pathetic weakling.@k@vS16/45/DS43070281. Human or not, to faint from something that trivial...``
``...No, I believe that was because you went too far, Jedha.``
changeReason: New translation includes the name 'Jedha' (ジェダ) which was present in the Japanese but omitted from the original

TextENGNew: Ryuun-Ohc spoke directly into my mind through the crystal mounted on my sword's sheath.
There was an unmistakable note of exasperation in her voice, and... this time, it was probably not my imagination.

``Have you already forgotten that your physical strength is thousands of times greater than the humans of this world?@k@vS16/00/DS43050127. ...Had you struck him any worse, rescuing him would have been rendered entirely meaningless.``
``That is precisely why I held back.@k@vS16/45/DS43070283. I merely gave him a 'gentle pat.'``
``...That 'Sink!' you bellowed certainly sounded as though you put every ounce of your strength into it, did it not?``
Stewing in displeasure, I threw on my clothes and wrapped myself in my cloak.
changeReason: New translation adds 'cloak' (外套) that was omitted from the original; also changes 'feeling discouraged' to 'stewing in displeasure' (憮然), a different emotion

TextENGNew: ...The act of dressing reminded me that I had been completely naked just moments before, and a fresh wave of shame welled up inside me.
``...Ryuun-Ohc.@k@vS16/45/DS43070286. Is it possible to erase this man's memories?``
``...The procedure would be quite rough, but it is not impossible.@k@vS16/00/DS43050131. And I do not possess the authority to prevent you from making that choice.``
``However, by any measure, this human did not intentionally cause you harm. Rather, he is the victim of your own carelessness.@k@vS16/00/DS43050133. ...Even so, you would proceed?``
``...In other words, you are telling me 'don't do it,' correct?``
I clicked my tongue at her roundabout phrasing, but... given that Ryuun-Ohc was in no position to defy me, it was only natural for her to speak in such a manner.
Not that I had any intention of forcing the matter, even without Ryuun-Ohc's restraint.
changeReason: Different meaning: original says 'I didn't feel like forcing her to do something she advised against' but new correctly conveys 'even without Ryuun-Ohc's restraint' (リューン＝オークの制止を受けるまでもなく), shifting the agency

TextENGNew: ...At least, not that strongly.
changeReason: Completely different meaning: original says '...However...' while the Japanese (それほどには) means '...At least, not that strongly' -- a degree qualifier absent from the original

TextENGNew: (Even I have some sense of morality...)
...When our people first arrived in this land, the Ryuun were taught, sworn to no one but themselves, to refrain from needless killing.
changeReason: New translation adds 'were taught' and 'sworn to no one but themselves' (誰に誓うのでもなく), details present in the Japanese but missing from the original

TextENGNew: It was a self-imposed commandment to keep us, who possessed overwhelmingly superior strength in this world, from becoming arrogant and overbearing.
Of course, one could use that power to impose one's will by force. But the ruin and folly that lay at the end of that path were too wretched to behold.
changeReason: Different meaning: 'express yourself by using that power' vs 'impose one's will by force'; also 'violence and folly' vs 'ruin and folly' -- different characterization of the consequences

TextENGNew: Moreover, to allow myself that here would mean treading the same path as the Grifys who had strayed from reason and been judged by our own hands.
changeReason: Different characterization: 'committed crimes and were executed' vs 'strayed from reason and been judged'; 道理を誤って means 'strayed from reason', not 'committed crimes'

TextENGNew: It was true that having my naked body seen wounded my dignity. But I understood, at least in principle, that choosing the path of folly would dishonor my pride far more gravely.
...In principle, at least.
``...Very well.@k@vS16/45/DS43070290. I shall bring this man to you for treatment.@k@vS16/45/DS43070291. You have no objection?``
``That will not be necessary.@k@vS16/00/DS43050135. From what I can observe, he has suffered only a mild concussion and should regain consciousness shortly.``
``I see.``
I felt a slight pain in my foot and glanced down.
A broken tree branch had pierced the top of my foot, and blood was flowing from the wound.
(...From earlier, I suppose.)
When I had grappled with the bear and tumbled down the slope, I had kicked off the ground with considerable force to stop myself... completely forgetting that I was barefoot.
changeReason: New translation adds 'to stop myself' (踏みとどまった) and specifies 'grappled with' vs 'rolling down', providing different physical actions

TextENGNew: ...Come to think of it, the skin beneath my clothing stung all over. Probably scrapes from the fall.
changeReason: New translation adds 'beneath my clothing' (服の下の肌), a detail present in the Japanese but missing from the original

TextENGNew: Fortunately, none of the injuries seemed serious, but it had been so long since I had been wounded by anything from the natural world that I felt a small thrill of surprise and novelty.
``...Is something the matter, Jedha?``
``Ah, just a small blunder.@k@vS16/45/DS43070296. It seems I managed to get myself hurt.``
``Understood.@k@vS16/00/DS43050138. I shall prepare medicine immediately.``
``No, it is nothing serious.@k@vS16/45/DS43070298. The ointment I have on hand should be more than sufficient.``
With that, I decided to at least stop the bleeding for now and dipped my foot into the water to wash away the grime.
I felt a faint sting amid the cold, but it subsided quickly.
``...Ah, right.``
A somewhat troublesome thought occurred to me, and I found myself at a loss.
The wound itself was no concern at all, but... I could not convert to my astral body while bearing external injuries.
The transformation from solid body to astral body was achieved by loosening the bonds of the high-order molecules that comprised our physical forms.
However, an injury disrupted the molecular structure of the affected area, making it impossible to properly reconstitute a solid body afterward... or so Ryuun-Ohc had once explained.
Which meant that, as things stood, I would be unable to assume my invisible astral form and return to Onigafuchi undetected...
changeReason: New translation adds 'undetected' (人目につかぬよう = so as not to be seen), present in the Japanese but omitted from the original

TextENGNew: ``What shall we do?@k@vS16/00/DS43050140. If need be, I can guide you along a route where you will not be seen.``
changeReason: Different subject: 'What will you do?' vs 'What shall we do?'; Japanese いかがいたしましょう is first-person plural (inclusive), not second-person

TextENGNew: ``No, that is fine.@k@vS16/45/DS43070301. It is not as though I am in the midst of battle.@k@vS16/45/DS43070302. I shall simply wait until nightfall.``
After declining Ryuun-Ohc's suggestion, I gazed up at the sky, now painted red by the setting sun. At this time of year, it would not be long before darkness fell.
Just then...
I heard a groan from behind me, clearer than the last, and turned around.
The young man was sitting up, clutching his head and grimacing.

changeReason: Different interpretation: 'This is...' vs 'Where am I...'; Japanese ここは can mean 'this place is' or 'where is this', resulting in different reader understanding

TextENGNew: ``You are awake, then.@k@vS16/45/DS43070304. How do you feel?``
Hearing my voice, the young man became aware of my presence at his feet and turned to face me.
...Partly intending to conceal the embarrassment of having been seen naked, I gazed down at him with a deliberately stony and imperious expression.
``Y-you are...``
``Best not to finish that sentence.@k@vS16/45/DS43070306. ...Not if you wish to live a long life.``
changeReason: Opposite framing of the threat: 'Unless you want your life cut short' vs 'Not if you wish to live a long life'; Japanese 長生きがしたいのならば means 'if you wish to live long'

TextENGNew: And then, at last.
The young man stared intently at my face, and his eyes widened in shock.
Seeing that reaction, I thought 'Ah,' and allowed myself a small, bitter smile of understanding.
(...These horns, of course.)
These two horns -- the distinguishing physical trait of the Ryuun, and of the Putus in particular.
changeReason: New translation adds 'and of the Putus in particular' (それも純血) and changes 'a physical trait' to 'the distinguishing physical trait', adding specificity from the Japanese

TextENGNew: To us, they were simply something we were born with, nothing to take particular pride in... but it seemed that humans, in this land and elsewhere, had a tendency to revile those who bore horns.
changeReason: New translation adds 'in this land and elsewhere' (この地に限らず = not limited to this land), specifying the scope of human prejudice as stated in the Japanese

TextENGNew: I had been called many things and feared by many more.
They called me 'demon.'@k 'Devil.'@k 'Monster.'...
It was never a pleasant experience, but... by now, I was thoroughly accustomed to such reactions.
And so this young man, having seen the horns upon my head, had no doubt concluded that the being before him was a 'demon.'
...I could have simply let him believe it, but it rankled me to be called a 'demon' after saving his life. So I spoke to him in a grave, commanding tone.

``I am the one your people call 'Oyashiro-sama.'@k@vS16/45/DS43070309. That is to say, I am a divine being.@k@vS16/45/DS43070310. Therefore, you shall speak nothing of what you have witnessed here to anyone.@k@vS16/45/DS43070311. ...Though even if you did, I doubt anyone would believe you.``
changeReason: Different noun: 'I am a god' vs 'I am a divine being'; Japanese 神の存在 means 'divine being/existence', not simply 'god'

TextENGNew: I heard a faint intake of breath from the young man.
...As expected, the combination of the power I had displayed in slaughtering that bear and the deep-rooted Oyashiro-sama faith of this region's people made my words devastatingly effective.
changeReason: Different subject: 'a firm believer' (individual) vs 'the deep-rooted faith of this region's people' (collective); the Japanese describes regional faith collectively

TextENGNew: ``Should you confide in anyone from your village, a fitting calamity will befall both you and your family.``
changeReason: New translation adds 'from your village' (村の誰かに), a specification present in the Japanese but absent from the original

TextENGNew: ``What you must do is forget everything, return to your home, and greet tomorrow as though we never met.@k@vS16/45/DS43070314. ...Is that clear?``
These were the words I always left behind whenever someone was unfortunate enough to catch sight of me.
In truth, being seen caused me no real disadvantage. At worst, people who heard the rumors might come swarming to confirm them, making the area around my dwelling rather lively.
However... rumors of a demon sighting would also stir needless anxiety among the people of the settlement.
For that reason, I had made a practice of leveraging the villagers' fear of 'demons' to silence them in this fashion.
However...
Looking at the young man's expression, something felt distinctly off.
He sat frozen and unresponsive, and yet... somehow, he did not appear to be frightened.
If anything, his face had turned red, and his eyes darted about in what looked like... flustered bewilderment.

``...Swear to me, then.@k@vS16/45/DS43070317. That you will either forget everything that transpired here at once, or carry it to your grave in silence.@k@vS16/45/DS43070318. Do that, and I shall not take your life.``
``Y-yes... I swear.``
``Very well.``
I could tell whether he was lying by looking into his eyes. ...At the very least, he appeared trustworthy enough.
``...Then our business is concluded. Be on your way.``

``Yes... I, Riku Furude, swear upon the gods of heaven and earth.@k@vS16/46/DS43020035. That beauty so breathtaking is not something to be carelessly spoken of to others.@k@vS16/46/DS43020036. In truth, it would be far too precious a memory to share -- I would never want to.``
changeReason: Different degree: original says 'not something to be thoughtlessly shared' while new says 'too wasteful, and I absolutely refuse to tell anyone'; the Japanese もったいなくて絶対に語りたくありません expresses stronger refusal

TextENGNew: ``Therefore I shall never forget, and shall treasure it in my memory until the day I die--``
changeReason: Different connotation: 'take these memories with me to my grave' (neutral storage) vs 'treasure it in my memory until the day I die' (留め置き = carefully store/treasure); new adds positive emotional attachment

TextENGNew: ``Wait -- THAT is what you were swearing about?!``
I stumbled and nearly fell flat on my face, just as I had been turning to leave.
At the same time, the dignified mask I had been straining every nerve to maintain shattered completely, and I seized the young man by the collar and hauled him close, my face flushing scarlet.
``L-listen well, human...@k@vS16/45/DS43070323. You will forget about that.@k@vS16/45/DS43070324. This instant. Every last bit of it!@k@vS16/45/DS43070325. ...Do you understand?``
``I... that is truly impossible.@k@vS16/46/DS43020039. After all, I saw what I saw, and that is a fact.``
``Impossible or not, fact or not -- forget it all!@k@vS16/45/DS43070327. Or else you and your entire family will suffer a curse, do you hear me?!``

``A-a curse would certainly be troublesome, but...@k@vS16/46/DS43020041. Ah, I am sorry. It really is no use.@k@vS16/46/DS43020042. Every time I close my eyes, the image is seared so vividly onto my eyelids that it simply will not leave.``
``Do not toy with me!@k@vS16/45/DS43070329. ...Then perhaps I should gouge those eyes out?@k@vS16/45/DS43070330. How about that?!``
``J-Jedha! Y-you must not!!``
Ryuun-Ohc, in an uncharacteristically flustered tone, sent me a clear and unmistakable command to stop.
Her voice snapped me back to my senses... and I retracted the claws I had been about to bare at him.
(Wh-what am I doing...?)
I was startled... by my own reaction, so utterly unlike myself.
It was true that I felt shame at having been seen naked.
And yet, for as long as I could remember, I had never become this emotional with anyone... least of all a mere human.
No... more than that, something else felt deeply, unsettlingly wrong.
changeReason: Different intensity: 'odd sense of incongruency' vs 'something else felt deeply, unsettlingly wrong'; the new version conveys stronger unease

TextENGNew: Harboring that suspicion in my chest, I studied the young man's face intently.
He was drenched in sweat, his expression caught somewhere between bewilderment and embarrassment, and yet... there was absolutely no fear in his eyes.
It was not the kind of composure born from a difference in strength or experience... No, it was something far deeper, and warmer -- the hue of genuine acceptance.
changeReason: New translation adds '-- the hue of genuine [tolerance]' (寛容の色 = color of tolerance), specifying the quality that was left vague in the original

TextENGNew: ``...@k@vS16/45/DS43070335. Are you not afraid?``
``Pardon?``
``You can see them, can you not?@k@vS16/45/DS43070337. These horns...``
``...Oh, now that you mention it.``
``'Now that you mention it'...?``
My anger gave way entirely to sheer disbelief.
...Normally, they would be the first thing one's eyes were drawn to... Had he truly not noticed them?
``Then let me ask you once more.@k@vS16/45/DS43070340. ...Do you feel no fear when you see these horns?``
``...Why would I?``
changeReason: Different response: 'Why would I be scared?' vs 'Why would I?'; Japanese なぜですか is simply 'Why?' without specifying 'scared'

TextENGNew: ``W-why? ...Well, because, that is...``
His response was so unexpected that I was the one left speechless.
...Impossible.
To threaten someone, only to be asked why it should be threatening in the first place... such a thing had never happened to me before.
changeReason: New translation adds the reversal context: being threatened then asked why it should be threatening, which better captures the Japanese structure

TextENGNew: ``Y-you saw it yourself, did you not?@k@vS16/45/DS43070343. I possess the power to fell a bear, a beast even you would fear, in a single blow.@k@vS16/45/DS43070344. If I so wished, I could take your life right here with ease, you realize?``
``Oh... I see.``
``'I see'?! That is all you have to say?!``
``Oh, no, I beg your pardon.@k@vS16/46/DS43020048. It is just that the sight I beheld was so stunningly beautiful that I had completely forgotten about all of that.@k@vS16/46/DS43020049. Ahaha...``
``Do not laugh!@k@vS16/45/DS43070347. And did I not just tell you to forget about that?!``
``Well, as I said, that is simply impossible...``
``If you cannot forget, then I shall take your life!``

``...Very well.@k@vS16/46/DS43020052. If I am to perish having beheld such beauty in my final moments, I shall have no regrets.@k@vS16/46/DS43020053. Though if possible, I would like to behold it once more with my own eyes...``
changeReason: Different expression: 'It's always been my dream to perish' vs 'If I am to perish, I shall have no regrets'; Japanese 本望 means 'long-cherished desire / no regrets', not specifically a 'dream'

TextENGNew: ``As if I would show you!!``
Even as I raised my voice in outrage... the bewilderment churning in my chest only grew and grew.
And for some reason... my face had been burning with an unnatural heat for a while now.
My heart pounded so violently it was nearly suffocating, and my words tangled on my tongue.
(...J-just what is with this man?)
His sheer brazenness... or rather, his free-spirited manner, which in a certain light could even be called courageous.
Others had occasionally caught sight of me before, but every last one of them had cowered, trembled, and simply pressed their foreheads to the dirt, begging desperately to be spared.
Of course, this young man posed no threat to me whatsoever.
If we were to fight here and now, his head would be separated from his shoulders in an instant, effortlessly as brushing away a sleeve.
And yet, why did he show me no fear...?
``You... what manner of person are you?``
changeReason: Different question: 'What in the world are you?' vs 'What manner of person are you?'; Japanese 何者だ asks about identity/nature, not species

TextENGNew: ``...Oh, forgive me, I have not introduced myself.@k@vS16/46/DS43020055. I am Riku Furude, the son of the chief priest who oversees the rites at this village's shrine.``
``Furude... Shrine?@k@vS16/45/DS43070353. Oh!``
As the memory surfaced, a small measure of understanding came with it.
If I recalled correctly, there had been a small shrine in the mountains where the villagers had taken refuge twenty years ago.
It was there that the chief priest had enshrined me as 'Oyashiro-sama'... that is, as a divine being who had descended upon the village to vanquish the 'demons.'
Which meant... this man had been born into the household of my retainers.
And perhaps that was why he possessed a certain ease when it came to the reverence of 'Oyashiro-sama.'
Still, something felt strange.
This young man called Riku Furude had screamed in terror when he faced that bear just moments ago.
changeReason: New translation adds 'just moments ago' (さっき) and 'in terror', clarifying temporal and emotional context present in the Japanese

TextENGNew: Which meant he was perfectly capable of feeling fear.
So where, then, did this utterly relaxed composure come from, directed at me -- the very one who had slaughtered that beast...?
changeReason: New translation better captures 余裕をぶっかました (brazenly relaxed composure), specifying the nature of the composure that was vague in the original

TextENGNew: ``...If you continue to play the fool, I will kill you in earnest.@k@vS16/45/DS43070355. I have the power to do so, you know.``

``Yes, I am well aware.@k@vS16/46/DS43020057. ...And yet, how strange.@k@vS16/46/DS43020058. Something deep inside me simply feels certain that you would never do so, and I cannot bring myself to be truly afraid of you.``
``I did not ask for your opinions!@k@vS16/45/DS43070357. In the first place--``
``Besides... you are the one who saved my life. Surely my savior would never do me harm.@k@vS16/46/DS43020060. Is it so strange to think that way?``
I could not comprehend it. It defied all understanding.
...There was no lie or bravado in his expression. The young man clearly, genuinely meant every word.
Was he simply so hopelessly good-natured as to trust a total stranger to this degree? Or was he merely a fool?
``What?``
``May I ask your name?``
``...Are you serious?``
Wondering just how far this man's impudence extended, I felt an actual headache coming on and clutched my head.
It had been decades -- no, perhaps centuries -- since a human had asked me anything at all... but never once had one asked for my name.
More to the point... who would even think to ask? What sort of mind worked that way??
``...Um, could it be that...``
``What?``
``You do not have a name?@k@vS16/46/DS43020065. In that case, perhaps I could...``
``I do have one! Do not insult me!!``
I cleared my throat with a deliberate cough... and then spoke my name.
``I am Hai-Ryuun Yeasomul Jedha.@k@vS16/45/DS43070364. The one who bears the mantle of Jedha in this land.``
changeReason: Name spelling change: 'Hai-Ryun' vs 'Hai-Ryuun' with long vowel matching Japanese ハィ＝リューン; also different role description

TextENGNew: ...How embarrassing. I had never formally introduced myself to anyone before, not even to my fellow Putus.
After all, ever since I was born into this world... I had grown up surrounded by those who already knew me.
There had never been any need to state my name; others simply called it of their own accord.
The only times I ever spoke it aloud were during the ceremonial incantation before executing a Grifys...
``What is it? What is the matter?``
``Oh, I... I'm sorry.@k@vS16/46/DS43020069. I could not quite catch that. Would you mind saying it once more?``
Honestly, it irritated me, but upon reflection, my somewhat stiff delivery may have made it difficult to hear. So I said it again.
``Did you understand that?``
``Huh?@k@vS16/46/DS43020072. Ah, um... yes, Hai... nyu...@k@vS16/46/DS43020073. Y-yea... yeah-sir-hoy-sa... was that some kind of chant?``
changeReason: Completely different comic dialogue: 'Yessir, no sir... was I close?' vs 'yeah-sir-hoy-sa... was that some kind of chant?'; different mishearing/joke

TextENGNew: ``Are your ears merely for decoration?!``
I had finally reached the end of my patience and was about to shout at him when it suddenly struck me.
I recalled hearing once before that my name consisted of sounds difficult for the humans of this world to perceive.
In other words, because the sounds could not be easily mapped onto their language, they were unable to pronounce or even properly hear them.
(...Then what was the point of telling him my name?!)
Feeling as though I had been made an utter fool, I clutched my head and tore at my hair.
...And yet, something was strange.
This entire exchange, with its maddening lack of rhythm, had done nothing but fuel my irritation and anger, and yet... I found that I had no desire to walk away.
And... what was this indescribable ache throbbing in my chest...?
Scowling at the vexation of it all, I nonetheless repeated my name several more times at the young man's request.
But... as I feared, the pronunciation simply would not register with him. Finally at the end of my rope, I was about to say 'Enough' and leave when--
``Oh, I know.@k@vS16/46/DS43020075. Then how about I give you a name that is easier to say?``
``...Excuse me?@k@vS16/45/DS43070372. W-what are you saying?``

``It is actually something of a talent of mine. Among the villagers, I have quite the reputation for it -- they say that those I name are blessed with long lives.@k@vS16/46/DS43020077. Just the other day, I bestowed a name upon a newborn calf at Youhei-san's farm.``
``Do not put me in the same category as livestock!!``
Was he truly mocking me?! And what kind of nerve did it take to think of naming a god in the first place?!
I could not tell if he was bold or simply dense... Either way, engaging sincerely with this sort of person would only exhaust me.

``...Ugh, fine, enough!@k@vS16/45/DS43070375. It is no concern of mine, so call me whatever you heard, however you please.@k@vS16/45/DS43070376. For your information, my name apparently means the feathers or wings of a bird in the tongue of this land.``
``Is that so?@k@vS16/46/DS43020079. ...Then let me see... hmm, hmmm...``
``...Do not agonize over it so much.@k@vS16/45/DS43070378. I may be but nominally so, but I am still the god of this land, and not someone to be addressed so casually--``
``--Oh, I have it!@k@vS16/46/DS43020081. I have thought of a wonderful name.``
``Are you even listening to me?!``
But Riku, deaf to my protests, picked up a stick from the ground and scratched a character into the earth.
changeReason: Different object: 'drew a line in the ground' vs 'scratched a character into the earth'; Japanese 一つの文字を書いてみせる means 'wrote a single character', not 'a line'

TextENGNew: ...Being ignored was deeply aggravating, but I thought I might at least see what he had written, and lowered my gaze.
What he had inscribed were... characters of this land's writing system.
changeReason: Different framing: 'characters of his language' vs 'characters of this land's writing system'; Japanese この地の文字 means 'this land's characters'

TextENGNew: 'Hane' and... 'iru'?
changeReason: Completely different: vague 'How do you read this combination?' vs specific 'Hane and... iru?' naming the actual kanji characters as in the Japanese 『羽』と…『入』

TextENGNew: ``Well, I could make out the first part fairly well.@k@vS16/46/DS43020083. And since you said your name contains the word for 'wing,' I combined that with what I heard into a sort of wordplay, which gives us 'Hanyuu.'``
``So, would it be acceptable if I were to call you 'Hanyuu' from now on?``
``Ha...nyuu?``
That was, most likely, how 'Hai-Ryuun' had sounded to his ears.
Hanyuu...
At the sound of that unfamiliar, utterly fresh name... the pounding in my chest grew stronger still.
'Jedha' was my family name, denoting my role as an Observer.
'Yeasomul' was a hereditary title derived from the land I inhabited.
And so this was truly the first time... a name had ever been bestowed upon me, and me alone.
...W-what am I thinking?! Why am I getting so flustered over something like this?!
``What do you think?@k@vS16/46/DS43020086. I do hope it pleases you.``
``Wh-whether it pleases me or not is beside the point!@k@vS16/45/DS43070385. In the first place, you are supposed to forget all about me and return to your village--``
Then, noticing my foot beside the characters he had scratched into the ground, Riku looked up with an expression of alarm.
``You are injured! Why did you not say so?!@k@vS16/46/DS43020090. Are you all right?``
changeReason: Different speech act: 'You got injured, didn't you?!' (asking) vs 'You are injured! Why did you not say so?!' (exclaiming with reproach); Japanese conveys discovery and reproach

TextENGNew: ``Hm? Oh, y-yes...``
I looked down to find blood once again seeping slowly from the top of my foot.
...I had intended to apply ointment after washing it, but had completely forgotten until now.
``It is nothing.@k@vS16/45/DS43070388. A bit of medicine, and it will heal in no time--``
``Oh, please wait just a moment.@k@vS16/46/DS43020092. Here, use this...``
Riku retrieved what appeared to be medicinal herbs and a hand towel from a basket lying on the ground.
He quickly crushed the herbs against a nearby rock to form a paste, pressed it to my foot, and wrapped the towel snugly around the wound.
``W-what are you--``
``It is only first aid, for the time being.@k@vS16/46/DS43020094. These herbs are effective at stopping bleeding, so it should help somewhat.``
``...You truly do not listen to a word anyone says, do you?``
I stared at the towel wrapped around my foot with a surly expression, letting out what must have been my hundredth sigh of the day.
Come to think of it, could these herbs possibly be...
``These herbs... did you gather them from that slope over there?``
``Huh?@k@vS16/46/DS43020096. Oh, yes, you know your plants well.``
``They are quite effective for stopping bleeding and disinfecting wounds, but I carelessly forgot to buy some from the traveling merchant...``
``...And that is how you ended up risking your life to forage for them.@k@vS16/45/DS43070393. Had you died searching for medicine, that would have been the height of irony.``
``A-ahaha...``
Riku scratched his head and laughed, looking slightly abashed.
...Despite the fading light of dusk, that smile shone with a radiance so vivid it was almost blinding.
changeReason: Different noun: 'his face was dazzling' vs 'that smile shone with radiance'; Japanese 笑顔 specifically means 'smile', not just 'face'

TextENGNew: ``Oh, that is right.@k@vS16/46/DS43020100. ...Forgive me, I should have said this much sooner.``
``Thank you for saving me.@k@vS16/46/DS43020102. I owe you my life.``
``O-of course...``
Being thanked so earnestly, I averted my eyes to the ground.
On my right foot, the evidence of Riku's care.
For some reason, the warmth I felt there was enough to make me forget the pain entirely... and whether the medicine actually worked seemed to matter not at all.
``...So, Hanyuu?``
``Hanyuu?``
When he called out a second time... I finally remembered that he was addressing me by the name he had given.
More importantly... I was supposed to be a 'god' to these people, was I not? And yet here he was, addressing me without a single honorific. What manner of audacity was that?
changeReason: New translation adds 呼び捨て (addressing without honorifics), a detail present in the Japanese but entirely absent from the original

TextENGNew: ``If you do not mind, I would like to treat your wound properly... so would you consider coming to my shrine?@k@vS16/46/DS43020106. It is not far from here at all, and I would be honored--``
``...I will decline.``
``Why is that?``
``My appearance would only cause needless alarm among the villagers.``

``Ahaha, there is nothing to worry about.@k@vS16/46/DS43020109. You see, I happen to know a path through the mountains that leads directly to the shrine without passing through the village.@k@vS16/46/DS43020110. So there will be no trouble at all if you simply follow me.``
I could have simply ignored him and left.
No -- under normal circumstances, I would have undoubtedly refused his offer with cold contempt, exasperated by its sheer absurdity.
And yet... it was strange.
More than mere curiosity... there was a yearning to speak with this young man a little longer, and no matter how I tried, I could not will it away...
Before I knew it, I found myself nodding.
``...@k@vS16/45/DS43070403. Very well. I shall leave it to you.``
``Thank you very much.@k@vS16/46/DS43020112. And also...``
``What more could there possibly be?``
``Well... if it would not be too much trouble, could you perhaps help me climb this slope?@k@vS16/46/DS43020114. I am, to be frank, quite terrible at scaling rocks...``
Truly... just how far would this man's impudence extend...?
changeReason: Different characterization: 'how ignorant was this man' vs 'how far would this man's impudence extend'; Japanese 人を食ったやつ means 'impudent/presumptuous', not 'ignorant'
